latest.title: Director/PDMR Shareholding
latest.content:
12 Feb 2026 16:30
RNS Number : 8253S
Savannah Energy Plc
12 February 2026
12 February 2026
Savannah Energy PLC
("Savannah" or "the Company")
Director/PDMR Dealings
Savannah Energy PLC, the British independent energy company focused around the delivery of
Projects that Matter
, announces that it has been notified of three transfers, totalling 91,805,655 Ordinary Shares in the Company (the "Ordinary Shares"), between companies beneficially owned by Andrew Knott, Chief Executive Officer and a Director of the Company, and a further transfer of 20,591,376 Ordinary Shares from a company beneficially owned by Andrew Knott to his personal name. Accordingly, there has been no change in Andrew Knott's beneficial ownership of the Ordinary Shares of the Company, which remains at 14.02%. The transfers have been undertaken to consolidate Andrew Knott's shareholdings in the Company into his personal name and a single company beneficially owned by him.
The information contained in this notification is disclosed in accordance with the requirements of Article 19 of the Market Abuse Regulation.

latest.title: On Market Share Buyback
latest.content:
5 Feb 2026 13:54
RNS Number : 9342R
Savannah Energy Plc
05 February 2026
5 February 2026
Savannah Energy PLC
("Savannah" or "the Company")
On Market Share Buyback
Savannah Energy PLC, the British independent energy company focused around the delivery of
Projects that Matter
, announces that the Company has today completed an on-market share buyback programme (the "Buyback Programme"). The Company may undertake further share buybacks opportunistically pursuant to the buyback authority granted by shareholders at the general meeting held on 28 November 2025, subject to the Company being in an open period and not being in possession of inside information and having regard to corporate liquidity and prevailing market conditions.
Under the Buyback Programme, the Company has repurchased a total of 32,063,641 ordinary shares in the Company ("Ordinary Shares") (the "Buyback Shares") at 6.8 pence per share for a total consideration of approximately £2.18 million. The Company confirms that all of the Buyback Shares will be cancelled imminently.
The Buyback Programme was conducted in accordance with the authority granted to the Company by its shareholders at the general meeting held on 28 November 2025, which permits the purchase by the Company of up to 318,098,135 of its Ordinary Shares.
Total Voting Rights
Following completion of the Buyback Programme and the repurchase and cancellation of the Buyback Shares, the Company's issued share capital will consists of 2,088,590,594 Ordinary Shares (the "Adjusted Share Capital"). The Company does not hold any Ordinary Shares in treasury and accordingly this will be the total number of voting rights and may be used by shareholders as the denominator for the calculations by which they determine if they are required to notify their interest in, or a change to their interest in, the Company under the FCA's Disclosure Guidance and Transparency Rules.
NIPCO Plc Shareholding
The Company notes that, as at 5 February 2026, NIPCO Plc ("NIPCO"), the Company's largest shareholder, is now interested in 631,479,202 Ordinary Shares representing approximately 30.23% of the Company's voting share capital based on the Adjusted Share Capital, including through Purebond Limited's holding of 1.7 million shares in the Company. The 30% threshold was crossed as a result of the Buyback Programme (rather than through NIPCO acquiring further interests in shares) and, accordingly, in accordance with Note 1 on Rule 37.1 of the City Code on Takeovers and Mergers (the "Takeover Code"). NIPCO is not required either now, or on completion of Unsettled Purchases (as defined below), to make a mandatory offer for the Company.
The current aggregate registered ownership of Ordinary Shares of NIPCO and Purebond represents approximately 27.93% of the Company's voting share capital based on the Adjusted Share Capital. The Company further notes that when NIPCO receives the approximately 48.15 million Ordinary Shares which have not yet settled pursuant to the acquisition of Ordinary Shares by NIPCO announced on 30 December 2025) (the "Unsettled Purchases"), the aggregate ownership of Ordinary Shares of NIPCO and Purebond will, following such settlement be approximately 30.23%.
However, as NIPCO is now interested in more than 30%, but less than 50%, of the voting share capital of the Company based on the Adjusted Share Capital, NIPCO (and any of its concert parties) may not acquire a further interest in shares with voting rights in the Company without incurring an obligation under Rule 9 of the Takeover Code to make a mandatory offer.
For further information, please refer to the Company's website www.savannah-energy.com or contact:
Savannah Energy
+44 (0) 20 3817 9844
Andrew Knott, CEO
Nick Beattie, CFO

company_30s.what_they_do: SAVE.L is an oil‑and‑gas exploration and production company that focuses on developing reserves in emerging African basins, most notably in Niger. They drill, test, and bring wells into production, selling the output to national and international energy markets.
company_30s.why_they_matter: The company supplies a portion of the world’s crude supply at a time when energy demand is high and many major producers are tightening output. Its niche focus on under‑explored African fields gives it a competitive edge over larger, more mature players, and the region’s political environment can create both risk and opportunity for those willing to operate there.
company_30s.current_state: SAVE.L is in the midst of a corporate reset. Recent filings show a share buyback, new non‑executive director appointments, and a partnership agreement with NIPCO Plc. The company is also announcing a joint broker arrangement and updating on its FY‑25 operations. These moves suggest a drive to shore up cash flow, reduce debt, and secure new funding or assets.

rns_narrative: The first theme is **governance and capital structure** – the company is reshuffling its board, buying back shares, and adjusting ownership stakes to align incentives and improve liquidity.

The second theme is **strategic partnership** – the deal with NIPCO Plc and the joint broker appointment point to fresh capital or operational support, potentially unlocking new exploration or production projects.

The third theme is **operational updates** – the FY‑25 update and the 2025 operational release give a snapshot of current output levels, cost structure, and future drilling plans, hinting at whether the company is stabilising its cash flow.

crowd_tape.dominant_theme: Quiet skepticism – the community is largely silent, with only a handful of posts. A few voices voice concern about the loss of the main Niger asset, while a couple of traders hint at upside if the partnership bears fruit.
crowd_tape.bull_narrative: A small group of bullish traders argue that the partnership with NIPCO could bring in fresh capital or access to new fields, potentially turning the company’s fortunes around.
crowd_tape.bear_narrative: Bears point to the recent loss of the Niger asset and the company’s thin cash position as red flags that could keep the stock trapped in a deep trough.
crowd_tape.sentiment_shift: Sentiment has barely moved. The partnership announcement sparked a minor uptick in bullish chatter, but overall the market remains indifferent, which is a good sign for a contrarian play.

whats_happening_now.attention_check: Google Trends is flat and dead, meaning no one is talking about the company. That gives the contrarian a surprise edge if a catalyst hits.

near_term_catalysts: A formal announcement that the partnership with NIPCO will bring a new exploration lease or financing package.
Positive drilling results from a new well in Niger or another basin that lift the company’s output profile.
A sharp rise in global oil prices (e.g., above $70 per barrel) that would boost revenue from existing wells.
Completion of the share buyback program, which would reduce the share count and potentially lift the price.
Any regulatory approval for the sale or transfer of the company’s remaining assets, providing a clear path to profitability.
expect_next.watch_for: Look for a volume spike that coincides with any of the above events, a break above the tight support zone that APEX shows, or a positive earnings release that beats expectations.
expect_next.timing_regime: The stock is still in the early capitulation phase – the price has been compressed for weeks, and the APEX score indicates a tight spring. A mean‑reversion bounce would likely occur once one of the catalysts activates and volume confirms buying interest.

buy_vs_sell.bull_case: If the price recovers to roughly halfway between the current trough and the pre‑crash level, the upside could be double or more. Historical RED‑type signals have delivered around 160% average returns when the crash was deep, and the current situation matches that depth. The partnership and potential asset recovery provide a clear path back to profitability.
buy_vs_sell.bear_case: The main downside is the confirmed loss of the Niger asset and the company’s thin cash position. If oil prices stay flat or decline, the company may not generate enough cash to service debt, which could keep the price trapped in the trough.
buy_vs_sell.risk_reward: Potential upside is roughly 200% if the stock rebounds to halfway levels, while the downside remains about 70% if the company fails to turn around. The asymmetry favors a contrarian long.

playbook.if_bullish: Enter when the price breaks the upper end of the tight compression zone with at least a 2‑to‑1 volume spike. Size the position so that a 10% stop‑loss would be comfortably below that support level, and let the trade run until the price hits the halfway mark or a clear reversal pattern forms.
playbook.if_watching: Hold a cash position or a small short‑term option spread until one of the catalysts above materialises. Trigger a buy when the partnership with NIPCO is announced or when drilling data shows a new well producing above 50 barrels per day.
playbook.if_passing: None identified at this time. The company has not shown any signs of imminent delisting or bankruptcy.

ai_insights.bottom_line: The stock is a deep‑crashed, silent setup. If the partnership with NIPCO unlocks new assets or funding, the mean‑reversion could deliver a 200% upside, making it a strong contrarian bullish play.
